simple_en: You deposit money in a bank for a fixed time and earn guaranteed interest
analogy: like lending money to the bank and getting extra in return
example: ₹10,000 at 7% for 1 year gives around ₹700 interest
reassurance: FD is considered one of the safest investment options

simple_en: The bank deducts some tax before giving you the interest
analogy: like tax being deducted from your salary before you receive it
example: If you earn ₹1000 interest, you may receive ₹900 after deduction
reassurance: You can claim it back when filing your income tax return

simple_en: Your bank deposits are insured up to ₹5 lakh by the government
analogy: like an insurance cover for your savings
example: Even if a bank fails, up to ₹5 lakh is protected
reassurance: This is why bank FDs are generally considered safe

simple_en: The time when your FD ends and you receive your money
analogy: like getting results after an exam ends
example: After 1 year FD, you get principal + interest
reassurance: You receive a fixed and predictable amount

simple_en: The original amount you invest in an FD
reassurance: Interest is always calculated on this amount

simple_en: The duration for which you keep money in an FD
analogy: like the length of a movie
reassurance: Longer tenure usually gives better returns

simple_en: Interest is earned on both your money and previous interest
analogy: like a snowball growing bigger over time
example: ₹1 lakh grows faster as interest keeps getting added
reassurance: Helps your money grow faster in long term

simple_en: Breaking your FD before the end date
example: 7% FD may give only 6% if broken early
reassurance: Useful in emergencies but may reduce returns

simple_en: Interest is added to your FD and paid at the end
analogy: like saving in a piggy bank
example: Interest keeps adding and final amount increases

simple_en: Interest is paid to you regularly
example: You receive interest every month or quarter
reassurance: Good if you want regular income

simple_en: A declaration to avoid TDS if your income is below taxable limit
analogy: like telling the bank you don’t need tax deduction
example: Used when total income is low
reassurance: Legal way to avoid unnecessary tax deduction

simple_en: Same as Form 15G but for senior citizens
analogy: a special version for people aged 60+
example: Used by senior citizens to avoid TDS

simple_en: A financial company that offers FD-like products but is not a bank
example: May offer higher interest than banks
reassurance: But does not have ₹5 lakh government insurance

simple_en: An FD that helps reduce your taxable income
FD automatically starts again after it ends
